description_long: This guard stone is a sculpted monument featuring three goats standing closely together on a rectangular stone base. The goats are rendered in a stylized, lifelike manner, with detailed facial features, horns, and legs, and are positioned so that one is in the center and slightly elevated, while the other two flank it on either side. The base is inscribed with green Chinese characters on the front face, adding a cultural and decorative element. The entire structure is made from a light-colored stone, likely marble or granite, and sits atop a square slab that serves as a foundation. The surface of the stone shows some minor discoloration and weathering, particularly near the base, suggesting exposure to outdoor elements. The overall form is solid and heavy, designed to serve as both a decorative and functional barrier in an urban environment.
description: A sculpted stone guard stone featuring three goats on a rectangular base with green Chinese inscriptions, made from light-colored stone and showing signs of outdoor weathering.
description_view_0: Front view showing all three goats, the central one elevated, and green Chinese characters inscribed on the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the profile of the goats and the layered arrangement of their bodies.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the three goats and the unadorned stone base.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the goats' profiles and the texture of the stone base.
attribute_location: Typically placed at the entrance of parks, temples, or historical sites, often near pedestrian walkways or at the edge of plazas to demarcate boundaries or provide symbolic protection.
attribute_installation_orientation: Fully above ground, upright, and securely anchored to a flat foundation slab.
attribute_wear_patterns: Minor discoloration and surface smoothing on the base, likely from weather exposure and occasional contact with pedestrians or maintenance equipment. Some dirt accumulation at the base and possible chipping on corners.
attribute_historical_markings: Green Chinese characters inscribed on the front face of the base, likely denoting a name or auspicious phrase.
